entries.text: Roll d20 ± ability modifier (abilities range from −3 to +3). Result equal to or greater than the DR: success. Creatures don't add any ability — they roll an unmodified d20 against the DR.
DR6 incredibly simple · DR8 routine · DR10 pretty simple · DR12 normal · DR14 difficult · DR16 really hard · DR18 should not be possible.

Each PC starts with Omens (amount varies by class; d2 without a class). Spend 1 Omen for one of: deal maximum damage with an attack · reroll a dice roll (yours or someone else's) · lower damage dealt to you by d6 · neutralize a Crit or Fumble · lower one test's DR by −4.
When Omens are depleted, roll the class' designated die (d2 if playing without classes) and regain that many Omens after resting at least six hours.

Catch your breath: restore d4 HP. A full night's sleep: restore d6 HP. Without food or drink, no HP is restored when resting; after two days a starving PC loses d4 HP per day. An infected character does not benefit from resting — instead, d6 HP is lost daily.
An infected character does not benefit from resting. Instead, d6 HP is lost daily until the infection is cured.
Same as tests. Roll d20 ± relevant ability vs DR set by the threat. Toughness vs poison/cold/heat/falling; Agility vs dodging; Presence vs Powers or charm; Strength vs grapples.
Roll d6: 1–3 enemies begin, 4–6 player characters begin. Individual initiative within the group: Agility + d6.

Melee: roll d20 + Strength vs DR12. Ranged: roll d20 + Presence vs DR12. A hit deals weapon damage. Enemies attack once per round unless noted otherwise. Players roll for both their attacks and defences — creatures and enemies don't roll dice in combat.
When attacked, roll d20 + Agility vs DR12. Success: avoid the hit. Fail: the enemy hits you and deals weapon damage (minus any armor).
Attack: ×2 damage, armor/protection is also reduced one tier. Defence: PC gains a free attack.

Attack: the weapon breaks or is lost. Defence: PC takes double damage and armor is reduced one tier. When armor is damaged, penalties to Strength and Agility tests are not modified. Armor reduced below 1st tier is ruined and cannot be repaired.
At 0 HP, a character is Broken. Roll d4 on the Broken table. Negative HP: dead.

1 — Fall unconscious for d4 rounds, awaken with d4 HP. 2 — Roll a d6: 1–5 = Broken or severed limb, 6 = Lost eye. Can't act for d4 rounds then become active with d4 HP. 3 — Haemorrhage: death in d2 hours unless treated. All tests are DR16 the first hour, DR18 the last hour. 4 — Dead.
Dead means dead. Negative HP: dead. Roll up a new scvm and walk into the next room.

Roll Morale (2d6) if: the leader is killed, half the group is eliminated, or a lone enemy has 1/3 HP left. If you roll greater than the creature's Morale value, it is demoralized — roll d6: 1–3 Flees, 4–6 Surrenders. '−' Morale means the creature never checks.
When meeting creatures whose reaction is uncertain, roll 2d6: 2–3 Kill!, 4–6 Angered, 7–8 Indifferent, 9–10 Almost friendly, 11–12 Helpful.

A few so-called Powers are known and they are usually found written in scrolls. When reading a scroll, test Presence DR12. If you succeed, the Power is activated and you subtract one use from your daily total. If you fail, the Power doesn't work, you lose d2 HP and you become dizzy for the next hour. During this time, Powers will always fail in the worst possible way.
A PC can use their Powers a total of Presence + d4 times per day. Roll the total amount every morning and choose from your available scrolls.
Scrolls will never work when wielding Zweihänder (two-handed) weapons or medium/heavy armor.

Powers are found written in scrolls. There are Unclean Scrolls (d10 table) and Sacred Scrolls (d10 table). To use a scroll, test Presence DR12. Success: Power activates, subtract one use from daily total. Failure: Power doesn't work, lose d2 HP, dizzy for an hour.

A failed Power use: the Power doesn't work, you lose d2 HP, and you become dizzy for the next hour. During this time, Powers will always fail in the worst possible way. A failed casting does not subtract from your daily total (only a successful use does).
The GM decides the effect on a Crit or Fumble. An optional table of arcane catastrophes (d20) is available for fumbles.

The GM rolls a die each dawn. A result of 1 activates one Misery. The die used is determined by the GM and the group — from d2 (end is nigh!) to d100 (years of pain). The GM then rolls d66 to determine which Misery occurs. The same Misery will not befall the world twice.
Years of pain: d100 · A bleak half-year: d20 · A fall in anguish: d10 · A cruel month: d6 · The end is nigh!: d2.

There are six Psalms (d66 table) of six verses each, plus the final 7:7. When Miseries accumulate, roll d66 to determine which strikes. The seventh Misery is always 7:7 — the world finally dies, the game ends.
The seventh Misery will always be 7:7. The seventh seal is broken for the seventh and final time. The game and your lives end here. Burn the book.

HP: hit points. Morale: 2d6 break number ('−' means never breaks). Armor (−dX): damage die subtracted from incoming hits. Attack: weapon name and damage die. Special: one or two abilities that feel genuinely horrible.
Roll 2d6: if greater than the creature's Morale score, it is demoralized — roll d6: 1–3 Flees, 4–6 Surrenders. '−' creatures never check Morale.
No Morale check ('−'). Many carry infection (Toughness test vs specific DR or sicken).
Described per creature. Always resolves as written. Always horrible.

1. Light (fur, padded cloth, leather etc) −d2 damage, 20s. 2. Medium (scale, mail etc) −d4 damage, 100s — DR +2 on Agility tests including defence. 3. Heavy (splint, plate etc) −d6 damage, 200s — DR +4 on Agility tests, defence is DR+2. Shield: −1 HP damage or have the shield break to ignore all damage from one attack. 20s.
On a critical hit (attacker natural 20) or fumbled defence (PC natural 1): armor/protection reduced one tier. Armor reduced below 1st tier is ruined and cannot be repaired.
Unarmed: d2 · Femur: d4 · Staff: d4 · Shortsword: d4 · Knife: d4 · Warhammer: d6 · Sword: d6 · Bow: d6 (Presence +10 arrows) · Flail: d8 · Crossbow: d8 (Presence +10 bolts) · Zweihänder: d10.

Battle axe: d8 · Bow: d6 · Club: d6 · Crossbow: d8 · Flail: d8 · Femur: d4 · Handaxe: d6 · Knife: d4 · Mace: d6 · Shortbow: d4 · Shortsword: d4 · Sling: d4 · Staff: d4 · Sword: d6 · Warhammer: d6 · Whip: d2 · Zweihänder: d10.
Shield reduces incoming damage by 1. Alternatively, you can choose to ignore all damage from one attack but the shield breaks.

You can carry Strength + 8 normal-sized items without a problem. After that, when testing Strength and Agility DR increases by 2 (from 12 to 14 etc.). It is impossible to carry more than twice Strength + 8.
Dried food 1s · Torch 2s · Night in hospice 3s · Steady meal 2s · Drink 1s · Shortsword 20s · Sword 30s · Horse 80s · Medium armor 100s · Heavy armor 200s.

Expensive, exotic, and very loud. All blackpowder weapons: Presence DR14 to hit · Ignore armor · LOUD (lesser creatures may roll Morale; others will investigate) · Most fire once then need d6+2 rounds to reload (cannot reload with enemy in melee range). Weapons — Pistolet: d6, one-handed, 80s · Culverin: d8, 120s · Arquebus: d10, 180s · Dragon: 2d4, two shots/load, full dmg in melee or 1 die to each of two adjacent targets at range, one-handed, 200s · Basilisk Gun: 2d10, requires two people, 300s · Blunderbuss: 2d6, two shots/load, full dmg in melee or 1 die to each of two adjacent targets at range, 250s · Heavy Arquebus: d12, two inventory slots, 240s · Pepperbox Pistolet: d4, fires 6 times before reload (each chamber reloads separately), one-handed, 160s · Ribauldequin: 6d6, 9 shots/load, attack every target in front then divide damage by total targets or 3 (whichever's higher, round up), d4+3 min to reload, 2 inventory slots, 600s · Cannon: 4d10, requires pack animal or very strong person to move, action to aim + action to fire (two characters may split), deals d8 to anyone directly behind it when fired, d4+1 min to reload, 1000s · Blackpowder Bomb: 3d4, roll another damage die on each result of 1 (repeat), 50s. Ammunition — 10 powdershots 50s · 10 basilisk shots 100s · 4 cannon shots 250s.
